violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as bagels) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Mushrooms and potatoes were observed held at 55-61 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food was placed on the counter 1.5 hours prior to inspection. Food was moved to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Macaroni salad was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and into portion cups with lids. Foods were made an hour prior to inspection and moved to the walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Soups and other prepared foods packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels without all the proper information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
